definition.name: extreme_point
definition.definition: A point x in a convex set K is an extreme point of K if x cannot be written as a proper convex combination of two distinct points in K: there do not exist y, z ∈ K with y ≠ z and 0 < λ < 1 such that x = λy + (1-λ)z. Equivalently, x is extreme if and only if F_K(x) = {x}, i.e., the face generated by x has dimension 0. For the state space S(A) of a C*-algebra A, the extreme points are the pure states, i.e., states whose GNS representation is irreducible (the commutant π_φ(A)' = C·I). The Krein-Milman theorem guarantees that S(A) is the closed convex hull of its extreme points. Critically, a state φ may be extreme in a constrained subset C ⊆ S(A) without being extreme (pure) in the full state space S(A).
